statistics.name: assists
statistics.description: The number of times a player who passes the ball to a teammate in a way that leads to a score by field goal, meaning that he or she was "assisting" in the basket. There is some judgment involved in deciding whether a passer should be credited with an assist.

statistics.name: turnovers
statistics.description: The number of times a player loses possession to the other team.

statistics.name: defensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the defense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot by the offense.

statistics.name: offensiveRebounds
statistics.description: The number of times when the offense obtains the possession of the ball after a missed shot.

articles.headline: Dallas visits Las Vegas after Bueckers' 27-point game
articles.description: Dallas Wings (11-6, 6-4 Western Conference) at Las Vegas Aces (12-5, 9-4 Western Conference)

articles.headline: New York visits Seattle after Malonga's 37-point game
articles.description: New York Liberty (12-6, 8-1 Eastern Conference) at Seattle Storm (3-15, 0-10 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Liberty's Ionescu has 1st double-double of year in win vs. Aces
articles.description: In a matchup of the past two WNBA champions, the visiting Liberty beat the Aces behind guard Sabrina Ionescu's first double-double of the season.

articles.headline: Liberty beat Aces 87-76 behind Breanna Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu
articles.description: — Breanna Stewart scored 15 of her 20 points in the second half and Sabrina Ionescu had a double-double with 16 points and 10 rebounds to lead the New York Liberty to an 87-76 victory over the Las Vegas Aces on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Hammon: Brunson opinion wrong, but won't apologize for take
articles.description: Aces coach Becky Hammon had a change of heart regarding her prior criticism of Knicks star Jalen Brunson, saying Tuesday night that her "opinion was wrong." But she's not apologizing for having it.

images.name: Why Stephen A. didn't see the current iteration of Jalen Brunson coming
images.caption: Why Stephen A. didn't see the current iteration of Jalen Brunson coming

articles.headline: 2026 WNBA championship odds: Lynx, Aces have shortest odds, just ahead of Liberty
articles.description: The Lynx and Aces have the shortest odds to win the WNBA championship, followed closely by the Liberty.

images.name: Natasha Howard of Minnesota Lynx [608x342]
images.caption: A strong start to the season has the Lynx tied with the Aces for the shortest title odds, just ahead of the Liberty.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: The Lynx have seen their odds shorten but have a tough schedule this week.
images.alt: Minnesota Lynx guard Olivia Miles (5) drives past Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers during the second half of a WNBA basketball game in Arlington, Texas, Thursday, May 14, 2026.

images.name: Olivia Miles [1296x729]
images.caption: Rookie of the Year favorite Olivia Miles is helping drive the early success of the Minnesota Lynx.

images.name: Paige Bueckers [1296x729]
images.caption: Paige Bueckers and her 19.3 PPG is a big reason for the encouraging start by the Wings.
images.alt: Dallas Wings guard Paige Bueckers (5) drives to the basket against Chicago Sky guard Natasha Cloud (9) during the second half at Wintrust Arena.

images.name: A'ja Wilson [1296x729]
images.caption: The Las Vegas Aces, led by reigning MVP A'ja Wilson, are again one of the favorites to win the WNBA championship this season.
images.alt: Las Vegas Aces center A'ja Wilson (22) celebrates a play with teammates during the first half of a WNBA preseason basketball game against the Dallas Wings in Austin, Texas, Sunday, May 3, 2026.

images.name: Satou Sabally [1296x729]
images.caption: The New York Liberty made some free agent moves, most notably signing Satou Sabally.

images.name: Angel Reese [1296x729]
images.caption: Angel Reese heads to Atlanta after two seasons in Chicago.
images.alt: Chicago Sky forward Angel Reese (5) dribbles against the Atlanta Dream in the first quarter at Gateway Center Arena at College Park.

articles.headline: Fantasy women's hoops: Teams with rising fantasy value, players to target
articles.description: Eight fantasy signs to watch, including which teams are abundant with fantasy value.

images.name: Sonia Citron [1296x729]
images.caption: Sonia Citron of the Washington Mystics has scored in double-figures in eight straight games.

images.name: Sydney Taylor [1296x729]
images.caption: Sydney Taylor of the Chicago Sky has 72 points over her past three games.

images.name: Aliyah Boston [1296x729]
images.caption: Aliyah Boston (right) of the Indiana Fever scored a season-high 34 points against the Chicago Sky

images.name: Nia Coffey [1296x729]
images.caption: Nia Coffey of the Minnesota Lynx had 15 points and five rebounds on Monday.

images.name: Maria Conde nails it from behind the arc
images.caption: Maria Conde nails it from behind the arc, 06/16/2026

articles.headline: WNBA Power Rankings: Atlanta on top after a week of chaos
articles.description: Atlanta is on top with a little help from Washington's unexpected three-game win streak and chaos elsewhere.

images.name: Sonia Citron [1296x729]
images.caption: Sonia Citron leads the Mystics with 17.6 PPG and 1.4 SPG. The second-year guard also averages 3.9 rebounds and 3.6 assists.

articles.headline: Caitlin Clark receives 5th technical as Fever hold off Mercury
articles.description: The Fever's Caitlin Clark earned her fifth technical foul of the season in Indiana's 86-77 win over Phoenix on Monday night, and later said the call was "ridiculous."

article.headline: Gabriela Jaquez has 20 points, 8 rebounds to help Sky beat Lynx 86-79
article.description: — Rookie Gabriela Jaquez set career highs with 20 points and eight rebounds, Kamilla Cardoso had 11 points and 12 rebounds and the Chicago Sky beat the Minnesota Lynx 86-79 on Sunday night.
article.linkText: Gabriela Jaquez has 20 points, 8 rebounds to help Sky beat Lynx 86-79

article.story: MINNEAPOLIS -- — Rookie Gabriela Jaquez set career highs with 20 points and eight rebounds, Kamilla Cardoso had 11 points and 12 rebounds and the Chicago Sky beat the Minnesota Lynx 86-79 on Sunday night. Rachel Banham scored 13 points, Natasha Cloud added 11 points and seven assists and Jacy Sheldon scored 10 points for the Sky (3-1). Courtney Williams made a 3-pointer and Natasha Howard followed with a layup that made it a four-point game with 1:44 remaining, but Jaquez answered 35 seconds later with a 3 that made it 83-76. Kayla McBride led the Lynx (2-2) with 20 points and Howard had 17 points, nine rebounds and seven assists. Williams also had 17 points and rookie Olivia Miles, the No. 2 overall pick in the draft, added 13 points and four steals. Chicago’s Rickea Jackson went down with a non-contact injury midway through the second quarter and did not return. Sydney Taylor made a 3 at the halftime buzzer that gave the Sky a 40-38 lead and they led the rest of the way. Jaquez made two free throws that capped a 12-4 run to open the third quarter and gave the Sky a 10-point lead. Skylar Diggins (eye) did not play for Chicago. Emma Cechova (right knee) missed her first game following a season-ending ACL injury Thursday in the third quarter of a 90-86 win over Dallas. Up next Sky: Play their home opener Wednesday against Dallas. Lynx: Host Toronto on Thursday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
